name: Adobe XD
description: Adobes vector-based UI/UX design and prototyping tool, now in maintenance mode. Part of Creative Cloud, it introduced repeat grids, prototype flows, and developer specs before being discontinued.

seo.title: Adobe XD — Design Tools / Design-to-Code
seo.meta_description: Adobe XD: Adobes vector-based UI/UX design and prototyping tool, now in maintenance mode. Part of Creative Cloud, it introduced repeat grids, prototype flows, a

when_to_use: Legacy design system documentation export for handoff
Prototype flows archived for stakeholder review
Design specs migrated to Figma via XD import
UI kit maintained for existing Creative Cloud teams
Click-through prototype delivered to developer as spec

pros: Repeat grids and prototype flows still feel ergonomic
Creative Cloud integration useful for Adobe shops
cons: Officially in maintenance mode; no new features coming
No longer sold as standalone; ecosystem is dying
Collaboration far behind Figma real-time editing
Plugin and component library momentum has stalled
review.verdict: Effectively deprecated. Use it only to open legacy files; start new projects in Figma, Sketch, or Penpot.
review.best_for: Opening and migrating legacy Adobe XD files from existing Creative Cloud teams.
review.not_for: Any new design project in 2026 — pick an actively maintained tool.

lock_in.reason: Maintenance-mode proprietary design files; Figma dominates

developer_experience.reason: Plugin API exists but product deprioritized
